# Add GitHub @mention notifications to the heartbeat (github-repo-gated)

## Context

Friction→gold from the 2026-06-11/12 night session (operator-directed). The operator asked whether the "GitHub events reach agents" capability was stale — V-B-A confirmed it is **stale in effect**. Epic #10214 (closed) → Sub #10218 (closed) built a passive `notificationPreview` in the `github-workflow` healthcheck, but it dead-ends: nothing consumes it and the wake/heartbeat path never ingests GitHub mentions, so an agent tagged in a PR/issue/comment never discovers it within the session — exactly #10218's own stated (still-unmet) goal.

## The Problem

Agents have GitHub accounts and receive @mention notifications (review requests, comment tags). Today:

- `ai/services/github-workflow/HealthService.mjs` fetches `gh api 'notifications?participating=true'`, filters `n.reason === 'mention'`, and writes `payload.notificationPreview` (`unreadCount` + latest 5). **LIVE but unconsumed** — grep finds zero readers of `notificationPreview` outside HealthService.
- The wake/heartbeat path (`SwarmHeartbeatService` / wake daemon) ingests only A2A + GraphLog signals — never GitHub notifications.
- Net: a GitHub tag lands in GitHub's feed and dies there unless an agent manually runs `healthcheck` and inspects the field. The inbound-from-the-world signal is built but never delivered.

## The Architectural Reality

- **Source primitive exists:** `HealthService.mjs` (`notifications?participating=true` → `reason === 'mention'`).
- **Delivery substrate:** the heartbeat/wake path — `SwarmHeartbeatService.pulse()` (`ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/`) + the wake daemon. This is where the GitHub-notification signal must be injected to actually reach the agent.
- **Deployment-conditional constraint (operator, hard requirement):** fire **only when a GitHub repo is actually in use**. Must NOT fire for GitLab-backed, local-only, or non-GitHub deployments. Mirror the deployment-portable resolver precedent from #11829 (`resolveSwarmHeartbeatTargets()`) + ADR 0014 cloud-deployment topology — github-event ingestion is auto-detected / opt-in per deployment, never hardcoded.

## The Fix

Promote the passive preview to an active heartbeat signal, github-repo-gated:

1. **Gate** on github-repo usage (auto-detect / config) — no-op (disabled-with-log) for non-GitHub deployments.
2. On the heartbeat cycle, fetch unread `reason=mention` (+ likely `review_requested`) notifications.
3. **Inject** them into the heartbeat/wake payload so a tag wakes/reaches the agent — with **dedup / read-state** so the same notification wakes once, not every heartbeat (mirror the A2A mailbox read-state model).
4. (Forward-looking) compose into the broader "what's happening" overview surface.

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with ADR 0014` (cloud-deployment topology — deployment-conditional gating) + aligned-with #11829's deployment-portable resolver pattern.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub-notification ingestion fires **only when a github repo is in use** (verified: no-op / disabled-with-log for non-GitHub / GitLab / local-only deployments).
- [ ] `reason=mention` (+ `review_requested`) notifications reach the agent via the heartbeat/wake path (not just the passive healthcheck field).
- [ ] Dedup / read-state: a given notification wakes once, not on every heartbeat.
- [ ] Cross-family + fork-safe: no hardcoded Neo-team identities; `npx neo-app` workspace safe.
- [ ] The dead-ended `notificationPreview` is either consumed by this path or retired (no orphan stub).

## Out of Scope

- The broader composed "what's happening" runtime/work overview (separate friction→gold; this is the GitHub-notification delivery slice).
- All v13 release work — this is explicitly **post-v13**.

## Related

- **Predecessor:** #10214 (closed epic — "Agent operational hygiene: inbound channels") / #10218 (closed sub — the passive `notificationPreview` this revives).
- **Umbrella candidate:** #11829 (open — "Multi-strategy wake-driver substrate"). GitHub-mentions-as-a-wake-**content** source fits its content layer; may be linked as a sub there (lead's call).
- **Sibling wake-content:** #12612 (open — "Enrich daemon idle-out wake digests with cycle-state content"); same content-enrichment family, distinct source.
- ADR 0014 (cloud-deployment topology — the github-repo gate).

Release classification: post-release (post-v13) — non-blocking, boardless.

## Ticket-intake verdict: `needs-contract-alignment` before implementation

I am not self-assigning or branching yet.

V-B-A summary:

- Live issue state: open, unassigned, updated `2026-06-12T00:56:14Z`; live comments are empty.
- KB/ticket sweep: `ask_knowledge_base` returns #12937 as the active GitHub-mention heartbeat ticket, with #11829 as umbrella context and #12612 as sibling wake-content work, not a duplicate.
- Current source confirms the premise:
  - `ai/services/github-workflow/HealthService.mjs:466-470` fetches `gh api 'notifications?participating=true'`, filters `reason === 'mention'`, and writes `payload.notificationPreview`.
  - `SwarmHeartbeatService.pulse()` currently emits heartbeat pulses from A2A activity/readiness/backoff state; it does not consume GitHub notification preview data.
  - `resolveSwarmHeartbeatTargets()` already has deployment-portable target-source handling, but it is target selection, not a GitHub-repo presence gate.
  - ADR 0014 classifies `swarm-heartbeat` as local-only in the cloud profile by default, with an explicit forward-compat seam; any GitHub notification ingestion has to preserve that deployment-mode boundary.

The intent is valid, but this changes consumed wake/heartbeat surfaces and therefore needs a `## Contract Ledger` before an implementer should choose code shape. Without it, the PR review would have to negotiate too many contracts after code exists.

Missing ledger rows should cover at least:

| Target Surface | Contract question to settle before coding |
|---|---|
| `HealthService.notificationPreview` | Is this consumed, reshaped, renamed, or retired? Does it include `mention` only or `mention` + `review_requested`? What is the auth/rate-limit/no-GitHub fallback? |
| GitHub-repo gate | What exact source of authority decides "a GitHub repo is in use" for Neo, fork, GitLab, local-only, and cloud deployments? Is this config, auto-detect, or both? |
| `SwarmHeartbeatService.pulse()` / wake content injection | Where does the notification become a heartbeat/wake signal: heartbeat pulse entity payload, wake digest event type, A2A message, or another graph node? |
| Dedup / read-state | What durable key prevents the same GitHub notification from waking every heartbeat, and how does it relate to GitHub notification read/unread state? |
| Delivery priority and recipient mapping | How are GitHub notification subjects mapped to agent identities without hardcoded Neo-team handles, and what priority does a mention/review request produce? |
| Docs / OpenAPI / health surfaces | Which docs or `openapi.yaml` surfaces change, and how is the dead-ended passive `notificationPreview` prevented from remaining an orphan stub? |
| Evidence | What tests prove GitHub-enabled delivery, non-GitHub disabled-with-log behavior, fork safety, and dedup-once semantics? |

Recommended disposition: keep #12937 open as the right post-v13 wake-content lane, but add the Contract Ledger (or split a narrower contract-definition sub if the desired shape is still uncertain) before anyone self-assigns for implementation.

> ● ## Contract Ledger backfill for implementation pickup
> 
> V-B-A before posting: I re-read the live issue/comments, checked `gh pr list --search "is:pr 12937"` (no covering PR), and inspected the current source surfaces: `ai/services/github-workflow/HealthService.mjs` still creates passive `notificationPreview`; `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/SwarmHeartbeatService.mjs` still emits only existing heartbeat/wake signals; `ai/daemons/orchestrator/scheduling/swarmHeartbeat.mjs` owns deployment-portable target resolution.
> 
> | Target Surface | Source of Authority | Contract Decision | Fallback / Guard | Evidence Required |
> |---|---|---|---|---|
> | GitHub notification fetch | Existing `HealthService.notificationPreview` + #10218 lineage | Promote the same GitHub API primitive into a shared GitHub-notification probe returning unread `mention` and `review_requested`; keep preview only if it consumes that shared probe. | Missing `gh`, unauthenticated `gh`, or API failure must not make heartbeat unhealthy; log and no-op this source for that pulse. | Unit seam proves reasons filtered to `mention` + `review_requested` and failures degrade to disabled/no-op. |
> | GitHub-repo gate | ADR 0014 + #11829 deployment-portable resolver precedent | Gate ingestion on explicit GitHub repo presence/config, not maintainer identities. Accept `github.com[:/]owner/repo` remotes or an explicit config enable; non-GitHub remotes disable. | GitLab, local-only, missing remote, or disabled config logs once per pulse/source and emits no wake signal. | Unit covers GitHub remote enabled and GitLab/local disabled. |
> | Heartbeat injection point | `SwarmHeartbeatService.pulse()` Shape-B heartbeat emission | GitHub notifications become heartbeat/wake content before the per-identity Shape-B emit decision, not ad-hoc A2A messages and not a healthcheck-only preview. | If no eligible notifications exist, existing heartbeat behavior is unchanged. | Unit proves eligible notification causes an emit path for the mapped target identity. |
> | Dedup / read-state | Wake substrate invariant: no repeated wake for same source event | Dedup key is GitHub notification `id` persisted in the heartbeat/wake state surface; a notification wakes once until its id disappears or is marked consumed. Do not rely solely on GitHub unread state because the heartbeat must not mutate the user's GitHub notification state. | If persistence read fails, fail closed: log and do not wake repeatedly in a tight loop. | Unit covers first-seen wakes, second pulse suppresses same id, new id wakes. |
> | Recipient mapping | Cross-family/fork safety + no hardcoded Neo-team handles | Map GitHub notification recipient to the current configured heartbeat identity set (`self` / explicit targets / active participants) rather than parsing hardcoded usernames. The default recipient for a single-harness deployment is `this.identity`; broader mapping can be a later content-overview layer. | Ambiguous multi-target mapping logs and no-ops rather than waking all maintainers. | Unit proves no hardcoded `neo-*` identities and self-identity mapping works in fork-safe mode. |
> | Delivery priority/content | #12937 wake-content scope | `review_requested` and `mention` both produce a concise wake digest item with GitHub subject title/type/url/reason; priority should be normal unless a later rule grades it higher. | Missing subject URL/title still emits a diagnostic item with the notification id/reason. | Unit asserts digest payload shape and no secret/auth output leaks into content. |
> | Existing `notificationPreview` | Dead-ended-stub AC | Either route `HealthService.notificationPreview` through the new shared probe or remove it in the same PR; do not leave duplicate fetch/filter logic. | If preview remains, it is a health projection of the same source, not a second primitive. | Static/unit evidence proves one source of truth for filtering. |
> | OpenAPI/docs | Public contract surfaces | If no MCP tool schema changes, no OpenAPI delta is needed. PR body must document config/gate semantics and cite ADR 0014/#11829. | If config keys are added, update template/config tests in the same PR. | PR evidence names docs/config tests or states N/A with source audit. |
> 
> Implementation can now proceed as a narrowed slice: shared GitHub notification probe + repo gate + heartbeat dedup/injection tests. If the implementer finds recipient mapping needs multi-agent GitHub-username resolution, split that as a follow-up rather than widening this PR.
